title: The History of Cycling
description: For questions 1–8, read the text below and choose which answer (A, B, C or D) best completes each gap.

content.text: The bicycle has (0) a long way since its invention in the early nineteenth century. The first version, known as the 'running machine', had no pedals and riders had to push themselves along with their feet. It wasn't until the 1860s that pedals were (1) to the front wheel, creating what became known as the velocipede. However, these early bicycles were uncomfortable and (2) to ride on rough roads. The introduction of the 'safety bicycle' in the 1880s (3) about a revolution in transportation. This design, with two equal-sized wheels and a chain drive, is still the (4) for modern bicycles. Cycling quickly became popular among all social classes and played a significant (5) in women's liberation, giving them unprecedented freedom of movement. In recent years, there has been a renewed (6) in cycling as cities struggle with traffic congestion and pollution. Governments are investing heavily in cycling infrastructure, and bike-sharing schemes have (7) off in urban areas worldwide. Whether for commuting, exercise, or leisure, the bicycle remains one of the most efficient and environmentally (8) forms of transport ever invented.

example.explanation: 'Come a long way' es una expresión fija que significa haber progresado mucho.

questions.explanation: 'Attached to' es el verbo correcto para describir unir físicamente una pieza a otra.
'Difficult to ride' es la colocación más natural para describir algo que cuesta hacer.
'Brought about' es el phrasal verb correcto que significa causar o provocar un cambio.
'Basis for' es la colocación correcta para indicar el fundamento de algo.
'Played a significant role' es una colocación fija para expresar la importancia de algo.
'Renewed interest in' es la expresión correcta para indicar un nuevo entusiasmo por algo.
'Taken off' es el phrasal verb que significa volverse popular o exitoso rápidamente.
'Environmentally friendly' es la expresión estándar para describir algo que no daña el medio ambiente.
